LK810: The Women's SuperPro React Polo is the second-highest-earning garment in our records — nearly 1,700 embroidered across 219 orders. It's the modern uniform polo: a smooth stretch knit that shrugs off wrinkles and snags through real workdays, in a genuinely tailored cut. Almost every order pairs it with the unisex K810.
K810: The SuperPro React Polo is Port Authority's do-everything uniform knit — nearly 1,300 through our embroidery heads across 213 orders. It resists the snags, wrinkles, and wash cycles that kill ordinary polos, which is exactly why office, medical, and field teams standardize on it. The women's LK810 completes the program.
K110: The Dry Zone UV Micro-Mesh Polo is the summer uniform workhorse — 74 separate orders here. Micro-mesh breathability, built-in sun protection, and a price that works at full-roster quantities. Landscapers, event staff, and golf outings keep it in constant rotation.
J325: The Core Soft Shell Vest is the layering half of our most-embroidered jacket line — 86 separate orders. Wind-blocking core warmth, arms free for work, and a chest panel that takes an embroidered logo perfectly. Companies running the J317 soft shell add this and let staff choose.
J754: The Challenger Jacket is a Port Authority institution — the classic two-tone company jacket that's been on Northwest loading docks for decades. Thirty-plus orders here; it's the jacket customers point at and say 'like that one, with our logo.'
J324: The Welded Soft Shell Jacket is the sleek one — welded seams instead of stitching for a clean, technical face that sheds wind and drizzle. It reads a tier dressier than the standard soft shell, which makes it a management and client-gift favorite.
L608: The Women's Long Sleeve Easy Care Shirt is the uniform button-down for the whole front office — wrinkle-resistant through a ten-hour day, in colors that match the men's S608 exactly. Nearly 300 embroidered here; it anchors our office uniform programs.
S663: The SuperPro Twill Shirt brings the React treatment to a dress shirt — stain-shedding, wrinkle-resistant twill that survives coffee, printers, and site visits. It's the button-down for teams whose shirts have to look pressed at 5 p.m.
S658: The SuperPro Oxford is the classic oxford weave with Port Authority's work-proof finish — stain release, wrinkle resistance, and an embroidered left chest that makes it uniform. Office and sales teams order it alongside the twill version.
L500: The Women's Silk Touch Polo is the tailored half of the most classic uniform polo in the catalog — 300-plus embroidered here. Smooth, forgiving, budget-friendly at roster quantities, and the color wall matches the men's K500 exactly.
K540: The Silk Touch Performance Polo upgrades the classic K500 formula with a wicking knit — same famously easy-wearing polo, better August. Over 300 decorated across 31 orders; teams migrating from cotton polos land here first.
K527: The Tech Pique Polo is Port Authority's modern pique — performance fabric with the traditional texture, so it reads classic but wears athletic. Two hundred-plus embroidered; it's the middle path when teams can't choose between old-school and technical.
K100: The Core Classic Pique Polo is the value anchor of Port Authority's polo wall — traditional pique, deep colors, full-roster pricing. Twenty-eight orders here; it's the sensible default when a company needs 40 polos that just work.
J335: The Hooded Core Soft Shell adds a hood to our most-embroidered jacket formula — 62 separate orders say Northwest crews appreciate the extra weather coverage. Same wind-blocking shell, same clean embroidery panel, drizzle-ready.
J706: The Textured Hooded Soft Shell gives the everyday company jacket a subtle texture that hides wear and reads premium — 47 separate orders here. The hood earns its keep in our climate; the chest takes a logo cleanly.
J354: The Challenger II updates the classic company jacket for its next few decades — same two-tone workhorse spirit, refreshed fit and materials. Fleets and crews migrating from the original J754 keep the same logo and nobody misses a beat.
K500P: The Silk Touch Polo with Pocket adds the detail trades actually ask for — a real chest pocket on the catalog's classic uniform polo. Forty-eight separate orders; we embroider beside or above the pocket and it reads shop-uniform proper.
K569: The Diamond Jacquard Polo weaves a subtle diamond texture into a performance knit — a polo that photographs rich without shouting. It's a step-up program pick for front-desk and sales teams; tone-on-tone embroidery finishes it.
K682: The City Stretch Polo is Port Authority's dressed-up modern knit — smooth, stretchy, closer to a golf-shop polo than a uniform. Companies pick it for customer-facing teams that need polish with range of motion.
L317: The Women's Core Soft Shell is the tailored cut of the region's default company jacket — 72 separate orders here. Same wind-and-drizzle shell as the J317, real women's fit, identical embroidery. Every mixed-crew jacket program should carry both.
K807: The Interlock 1/4-Zip is the knit quarter-zip at a polo price — smooth interlock fabric with a zip collar, the easy layering piece for office programs. Embroiders sharp on the chest panel.
J851: The Packable Puffy Vest packs into its own pocket and rides behind a truck seat until the temperature drops — 42 separate orders, many as gifts. Light insulation, modern quilting, and a clean chest panel for the logo.
L850: The Women's Packable Puffy Jacket is the tailored version of the stash-it-anywhere insulated layer — a company-gift regular that actually gets worn. Quilted warmth, packable convenience, embroidered chest.
J331: The All-Conditions Jacket earns its name — a waterproof shell with a zip-out fleece liner that covers three Northwest seasons in one garment. Sixty-two separate orders; it's the one-jacket answer for crews that face everything.
W668: The Plaid Flannel Shirt posted a pattern we rarely see: 114 separate orders for 161 units — people buy this one deliberately, one and two at a time. It's a real flannel with an embroidered chest logo that turns Pacific Northwest casual into company wear.
JP54: The Competitor Jacket is the classic coach's shell — lightweight, wind-shedding, with that unmistakable warm-up look. Thirty-one orders here, mostly teams and schools; a left-chest logo plus back print completes it.
J920: The Collective Tech Outer Shell is the flagship of Port Authority's modern outerwear system — a clean technical shell built to layer over the Collective insulators. It's the current-generation company jacket for brands that want 2020s, not 1990s.
SRJ754: The Enhanced Visibility Challenger takes the classic company jacket and adds hi-vis paneling and reflective accents — visibility without full ANSI regalia. Crews that work around traffic but don't need certified Class 3 pick it; 19 orders and climbing.
J304: The All-Season II is the fleece-lined workhorse jacket — a weather shell with built-in warmth at a fleet-friendly price. Forty-two separate orders; delivery fleets and facility crews are the regulars.
K455: The Rapid Dry Polo is the classic moisture-managing pique — a longtime uniform standby that dries fast and holds its color through industrial wash cycles. Twenty orders here; it's the proven-safe choice.
K525: The Dry Zone Ottoman Polo pairs the wicking Dry Zone fabric with a distinctive ottoman rib texture — a uniform polo with visible quality. Restaurants and service teams that want texture without pattern pick it.
K585: The Shadow Stripe Polo carries a tonal self-stripe that reads solid from a distance and detailed up close — 171 units moved here in just two orders. It makes even a simple left-chest logo look considered.
LK110: The Women's Dry Zone UV Micro-Mesh Polo is the tailored cut of our summer uniform staple — 33 separate orders. Same breathable, sun-protective knit as the K110, real women's fit, matching colors for full-team programs.
J407: The Essential Rain Jacket is exactly what the Northwest ordered — a clean, genuinely rain-ready shell at a whole-crew price. Twenty-two orders in short order; it's becoming our default answer to 'we need rain jackets with the logo.'
A702: The Easy Care Waist Apron with Stain Release is the front-of-house workhorse — nearly 400 embroidered, mostly for restaurants, taprooms, and coffee shops. Three pockets, stain-shedding fabric, and a logo placement that faces every customer at the table.
